title: JACKSON STATE UNIVERSITY 2023 A Day With Da Boom [NOLA] Highlight Reaction Review | Steven Holiday
body:
There is NO SUCH THING as a smiling percussionist in a marching band... LOL!! Heck, I can't remember a single band member smiling while in marching band mode.

To answer your question, the hardest working part of the drum section in my opinion is the snares.
author: @GVocalz3

title: JACKSON STATE UNIVERSITY 2023 A Day With Da Boom [NOLA] Highlight Reaction Review | Steven Holiday
body: The 6:33 joint sounds good; but I believe I heard TxSU play it during the Fall. I believe Pollard arranged it for TxSU. Both sound good however.
author: @edwinmoore357

title: JACKSON STATE UNIVERSITY 2023 A Day With Da Boom [NOLA] Highlight Reaction Review | Steven Holiday
body: If i had to pick a piece of the percussion that does the most work based on experience, id say either bass or cymbals. Now bases, becuse of constantly having to deal with both the weight of the drum, and constant exertion of playing for some songs. And cymbals, because of both cutting yourself half the time, or just adjusting to either the weight, or movement style.
author: @saiah_yt795

title: JACKSON STATE UNIVERSITY 2023 A Day With Da Boom [NOLA] Highlight Reaction Review | Steven Holiday
body: The band is superb, but, I'm sorry to say that the Jsettes are not on the same level of excellence as the band. Unfortunately, the Jsettes dance style and routines are not polished and classy, and they are redundant. However, the girls are beautiful, but should invest in dance lessons.

title: Michael Jackson - The Lady in My Life (Audio)
body:
“The Lady in My Life” was one of the most difficult tracks to cut. We were used to doing a lot of takes in order to get a vocal as nearly perfect as possible, but Quincy wasn’t satisfied with my work on that song, even after literally dozens of takes. Finally he took me aside late one session and told me he wanted me to beg. That’s what he said. He wanted me to go back to the studio and literally beg for it. So I went back in and had them turn off the studio lights and close the curtain between the studio and the control room so I wouldn’t feel self-conscious. Q started the tape and I begged. The result is what you hear in the grooves."

Jackson, Michael. Moonwalk: A Memoir (pp. 197-198). Crown.
author: @priscilacosta5965

author: @terrenceharris-hughes4436

title: THIS IS WHY 5-STAR RECRUITS ARE COMMITTING TO JACKSON STATE.. (DEION SANDERS)
body: It ain't no lsu or Miami or Oklahoma but it's more than that.   All that money and shit is great,  but having a living legend coaching at js is motivation for a generation or more of some of the most elite talent to be brought in to js.  They are choosing a road that is different.  They are choosing the possibility of greatness over a promise of come here and get lots of shit and hope you are stand out enough to be position 1 or 2. Learning directly from the mouth of a goat who can relate to these young men on different levels, and get thru to them and speak In a way they respect and understand is a huge opportunity. I'm sure many will go on to play at giant d1 machines and if they do they will have a huge step up over many of the dudes who chose a direct path to d1. It's am experience of a life time for guys like Travis.  He can look at highlights and see what coach did on the field and understand coach wants what's best and knows how to get there if I listen and do what he says. It's different when you have a giant staff and let's say an older white coach who's more hard-nosed and doesn't have the same pedigree as prime.  It would be a harder sell in that regard.  You would be looking more at the team and the guys who got recruited  and the campus than anything I'd think.  Its great.  Good for Travis, hope he's able to hang in there put on a few lbs of muscle and do what he does.
author: @Hustler_state

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body:
I leave for basic in 6 days, although I'm pretty nervous I can't wait to experience it & become a better me.
(Update 1): Finally graduated BCT a few days ago, I am now in Arizona for AIT.

(Update 2): Done with AIT. Graduated damn near top of my class. Off to Airborne School.

(Update 3): Finished airborne a while ago, now to wait out my contract lol. It’s all relatively easy in retrospect, just do the right shit at the right time in the right uniform.

(Update 4): I have maybe like, 18 months until the end of my contract? Roughly? Time flies.

(Update 5:) One year left, in the TAP (Transition Assistance Program) process as of current. (or so I thought)

(Update 6:) Forgot about this comment for a while, but I just reenlisted for 4 in Hawaii. Probably gonna stay in for a few contracts tbh. (This was June of 2025 roughly, I think.)

(Update 7, since a lot of you wanted some more. Guess I gotta keep this going as a little journal for you guys or something) Currently February 11th, 2026, I shipped out for basic on January 24th, 2023. I'm on my second contract in Hawaii now, I love it here aside from all the traffic. Told myself and everyone else I wasn't gonna do a second but here I am. Have my SGT promotion board next week, got about a week and a half notice about it so I've been studying my ass off. I'll probably forget about this comment for awhile, so, see you guys next update.
author: @SampsonWrestling

title: Basic Training Day in the Life

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: My youngest is two weeks in she left one week after graduation. I was so nervous  at first. I would say I'm the most proudest mother ever. From these  two weeks , I can hear the growth , confidence, and structure  that it has given her already. I am very grateful for her decision 🙏🏾 💕
author: @Kaym-q7l

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: I leave to basic in about a month and a half. Never been more excited to get my ass beat into shape in my whole life. I haven’t done shit with myself since I graduated high school 7 years ago. I entered the workforce right after graduating, Worst mistake of my life. Now i plan on correcting the mistake. I’m finally going to make my best effort to be the best I can be. 11x Airborne Infantry.
author: @WillGarett

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: Graduated in November from ft Jackson. The best 10 weeks of my life that I never want to do again 😂😂😂

title: Basic Training Day in the Life

I'm excited and scared at the same time because I'm leaving my 2 year old daughter but I know it's for the best for her. God in charge. I have this YouTube channel but when I get back I'm going to think about creating one just to talk about my experiences in the National Guard. I’m ready to go yeah ❤😂

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body: I went to basic in 1979, scared to death. I was in the last OSUT unit at Ft Gordon. I went from being a civilian  executive secretary to the Distinguished Honor graduate! I proudly wear my veteran t shirts. Best part: when I came home and saw my dad's face! And then watching him trying to pick up his 5'3" soldier daughter's full duffle bag!!❤. Proud to be a US Army Veteran! ❤️🇺🇸 Keep it strong!
author: @reppi8742

title: Basic Training Day in the Life
body:
Very glad for their dedication to join the military family, striving together, training together, and having each other's back no matter what . Best family ever.
Shaves the scalp, exercises , pushes limits , goes through obstacles, goes through the gas chamber, takes apart artillery , and learns to shoot , Marches together, and strives as a family.
Very glad for their oppertunities, stay strong warriors !

title: The College Tour - Jackson State University
body:
Former great NY JETS 4X PRO BOWLER, Rich Caster #88, was a graduate of JSU and brought me , out of curiosity, to this fine video.
Sadly, he passed Feb 2024 at the age of 75 yrs. But his love and legacy for this school shall remain forever.
author: @jessedylan6162
